prep.summary: Review of your work notes reveals no direct historical records for Ray—however, your May 19 sync with Mark serves as the primary baseline for this discussion. This session established critical transition plans—specifically moving Vince into Matt's vacated role to maintain engagement—and outlined the structural format changes for the upcoming Horizontal Planning meeting. You also tracked key project updates—specifically the 2031 timeline for TRAD Plus and reference value migrations for DAMIT—alongside Mark's personal health update.
tasks: Confirm the official transition timeline and workload distribution for Vince taking over Matt's architecture responsibilities.
Establish coverage protocols and communication channels for Mark's upcoming absence during his medical treatment.
Review the final agenda for Thursday's Horizontal Planning meeting to prepare the core architecture team for their observer role.
Verify the scope and tracking schedule for the TRAD Plus Guidewire migration and the DAMIT pricing revamp.
prep.title: Sync with Mark | Ray

prep.summary: You lack existing work notes—or past meeting records—for your monthly touchpoint with Ian Benoist. You must use this session to reconstruct recent touchpoint outcomes—and establish a clean slate for tracking ongoing action items. Focus the discussion on immediate calendar bottlenecks—and define the exact support Ian requires from you this month.
tasks: Request a debrief on the outcomes of your last discussion with Ian—noting any verbal agreements or pending deliverables.
Create a dedicated tracking document in your Drive folder—ensuring future meeting takeaways are systematically logged.
Review Ian's calendar for the next thirty days—identifying high-priority conflicts that require immediate resolution.
Establish a regular cadence for agenda inputs—minimizing prep time before each monthly touchpoint.
prep.title: Monthly EA Touchpoint - Ian Benoist

prep.summary: You met with Jonathan and Mark on May 18th to address the tactical bottlenecks of managing influxes of AI requirements—specifically relying on temporary prompts rather than a sustainable system. Jonathan committed to utilizing his Snowflake warehouse access to pull change records—allowing him to leverage an architecture checklist to analyze data quality. Your ultimate goal remains automating this validation process—using batch jobs to flag low-quality records before human intervention is required.
tasks: Confirm Jonathan's progress on pulling Snowflake change records—verifying if the architecture checklist is ready for data quality analysis.
Establish a timeline to replace the unsustainable prompt process—transitioning to structured feedback spreadsheets to handle the requirements influx.
Explore integration opportunities with Andy Napalese's Foundry framework—focusing on bridging the gap between high-level business cases and Jira implementation.
Draft the automated batch-job requirements—aiming to flag incomplete domain-level Change Review Group records prior to manual review.
prep.title: Bi-weekly 1:1 w/Jonathan N | Mark F
